threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the bus lanes when cycling – locals weave through like it's an obstacle course. Welcome to your new reality!
Last night on the N68, a lad serenaded the whole bus with mum's old soul records. Best night bus ride in ages—definitely a Brixton vibe!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the guy walking a ferret on a leash past the Turkish bakery. That’s Neukölln for you, embracing the quirky side of life.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a spontaneous street concert that turns into a dance-off with no one really organizing it. Pure Berlin magic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they instinctively yell at BART for delays, like it’s a personal betrayal, not just a bad day.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 while a jazz band set up in the middle of the street. Pure SF moment—traffic halted, but not the vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the local bars serve the same tapas, but only a few know about the hidden gem on Carrer de Verdi with its unique family recipes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their struggle with the metro maps; they keep staring at them like they’re deciphering ancient runes.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' like it’s the only line that matters. Everyone knows Dalston's a bus ride away from adventures.
Caught a guy on the 37 last night who brought his sax and played smooth jazz. Bus went quiet, even the usual drunks stopped to listen. Just London, innit?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a saxophone echoing from the courtyard while kids played and an old lady fed pigeons. That's Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can dance on a rooftop in Kreuzberg during a festival, drink a beer, and listen to a 90s techno DJ while fireworks go off. Unique vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the best tacos are hidden in a taco truck on 16th, not in the fancy spots on Valencia. It’s a game changer.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 tonight, and a guy was shouting Shakespeare while balancing on one leg. Only in the Tenderloin, right? Muni’s the real stage.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all tapas are the same; they miss how each bar has its own secret recipes and that bar staff can be quite territorial about them.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they keep looking at the metro map like it’s a treasure map—confusion mixed with hope, especially at the Passeig de Gràcia stop.
